expect: Wrapper/runtime portability path will surface >=1 explicit git read-tree HEAD recovery hint for PowerShell index/tree corruption failures, and >=2 targeted regression tests will pass.
actual: tools/check.ps1 now captures native command output from both the bash-backed and python fallback paths, detects git-index/tree-corruption signatures, and emits a concrete WSL recovery command (`rm -f .git/index.lock && git read-tree HEAD && git update-index --refresh`) before exiting. tools/maintenance_inventory.py now surfaces the same recovery path during WSL /mnt PowerShell startup checks. Targeted regressions passed: runtime portability emits the recovery hint on WSL+pwsh, and the wrapper source contains the recovery command.
diff: Expectation matched. The recovery hint landed in both the reactive path (wrapper failure handling) and the proactive path (startup portability notice). The only gap vs a fuller hardening pass is that the PowerShell wrapper still relies on signature matching rather than a deeper git-health probe, but the explicit repair path is now surfaced where SIG-119 identified the blind spot.
